generatedSentence: Evelyn, a worldly former golf player with a self-inflicted handicap of solitude, spent her anniversary at a secluded country salon, sipping cider under circular umbrellas. A lyrical melancholia, born of dryness and the slow decay of her spirit, left her feeling utterly unlucky, her life a monotone rhythm just slightly off kilter. Nevertheless, the reedy whispers of privacy and the familiar jostle of the hallway leading to the manager's office, where she sought nervine calm, brought a strange sense of knowledgeable peace to her continental heart. The sudden news that her old business partner had been highjacked and accused of being a merchant marauder sent a nervous tremor through her. Evelyn understood there was a mystery she couldn't misunderstand, a tyrannical force at play that would afflict them all. This was no typical university prank, but a fanatical plot that felt far too serious; a valkyrie-like figure, a jester-esque heathen, seemed to conceive a plan to kill and sucking their resources, threatening to overcook the whole operation. Her instincts screamed obstacle, demanding she advance as she felt her entire world disjointing, like a machine suddenly jackknifed, its numeral gears seizing with unbearable torque from a ganging conspiracy. In the industrial graveyard of an old train yard, amidst towering locomotives and piles of landfill junk, an irresistible urge to confront the source of the chaos took hold. A sudden fire erupted, forcing her to instinctively showjumped over a burning wreckage, the baroque twisting metal like a monstrous sculpture. She felt the giveback of her old training, her knuckle cracking as she delivered a precise blow, watching a hidden attacker get toppled from a plinth-like pile of debris, their jaw clenching in surprise. Under the aegis of the night, she saw the unitary purpose in her fight, determined to remain uninjured, battling her way through the trench-like alleys, the air thick with the smell of smoke and the promise of victory. She used a broken toothpicks-like shard of metal as a makeshift weapon, leveraging its benefit, ready to rise, dressed in a tough gabardine coat, in this international naval affair. After the chaos, a quiet sense of utopia settled. Evelyn sat in her study, a lone candelabrum casting flickering shadows over her tarot cards and scattered newspapers, sifting through the nuance of the recent events. She understood the deeper instructions now, feeling keen and resolute for the future. Her movements, once tentative, now had the fluid locomotion of her past kickboxing glory. She decided to postpone retirement, finding new purpose, a panegyric to resilience. The veiling of the past was lifted, revealing a future she could find and approve, neither a complete victory nor a total defeat, just an outtake from a larger story. She peeled a chicken off a rotisserie, considering the future, her old sweater worn and comforting, the puzzle of the past a resolved jigsaw, leaving only the comforting nubble of certainty and the protective netting of her renewed strength, ready to crawl through any obstacle, even if it meant becoming a lumberjacks-level problem solver.
panelSentences: Evelyn, a worldly former golf player with a self-inflicted handicap of solitude, spent her anniversary at a secluded country salon, sipping cider under circular umbrellas. A lyrical melancholia, born of dryness and the slow decay of her spirit, left her feeling utterly unlucky, her life a monotone rhythm just slightly off kilter. Nevertheless, the reedy whispers of privacy and the familiar jostle of the hallway leading to the manager's office, where she sought nervine calm, brought a strange sense of knowledgeable peace to her continental heart.

The sudden news that her old business partner had been highjacked and accused of being a merchant marauder sent a nervous tremor through her. Evelyn understood there was a mystery she couldn't misunderstand, a tyrannical force at play that would afflict them all. This was no typical university prank, but a fanatical plot that felt far too serious; a valkyrie-like figure, a jester-esque heathen, seemed to conceive a plan to kill and sucking their resources, threatening to overcook the whole operation. Her instincts screamed obstacle, demanding she advance as she felt her entire world disjointing, like a machine suddenly jackknifed, its numeral gears seizing with unbearable torque from a ganging conspiracy.

In the industrial graveyard of an old train yard, amidst towering locomotives and piles of landfill junk, an irresistible urge to confront the source of the chaos took hold. A sudden fire erupted, forcing her to instinctively showjumped over a burning wreckage, the baroque twisting metal like a monstrous sculpture. She felt the giveback of her old training, her knuckle cracking as she delivered a precise blow, watching a hidden attacker get toppled from a plinth-like pile of debris, their jaw clenching in surprise. Under the aegis of the night, she saw the unitary purpose in her fight, determined to remain uninjured, battling her way through the trench-like alleys, the air thick with the smell of smoke and the promise of victory. She used a broken toothpicks-like shard of metal as a makeshift weapon, leveraging its benefit, ready to rise, dressed in a tough gabardine coat, in this international naval affair.

After the chaos, a quiet sense of utopia settled. Evelyn sat in her study, a lone candelabrum casting flickering shadows over her tarot cards and scattered newspapers, sifting through the nuance of the recent events. She understood the deeper instructions now, feeling keen and resolute for the future. Her movements, once tentative, now had the fluid locomotion of her past kickboxing glory. She decided to postpone retirement, finding new purpose, a panegyric to resilience. The veiling of the past was lifted, revealing a future she could find and approve, neither a complete victory nor a total defeat, just an outtake from a larger story. She peeled a chicken off a rotisserie, considering the future, her old sweater worn and comforting, the puzzle of the past a resolved jigsaw, leaving only the comforting nubble of certainty and the protective netting of her renewed strength, ready to crawl through any obstacle, even if it meant becoming a lumberjacks-level problem solver.
rawPanelSentences: Evelyn, a **worldly** former **golf** **player** with a self-inflicted **handicap** of solitude, spent her **anniversary** at a secluded **country** **salon**, sipping **cider** under **circular** **umbrellas**. A **lyrical** melancholia, born of **dryness** and the slow **decay** of her spirit, left her feeling utterly **unlucky**, her life a **monotone** rhythm just slightly off **kilter**. **Nevertheless**, the **reedy** whispers of **privacy** and the familiar **jostle** of the **hallway** leading to the **manager**'s office, where she sought **nervine** calm, brought a strange sense of **knowledgeable** peace to her **continental** heart.

The sudden news that her old business partner had been **highjacked** and accused of being a **merchant** **marauder** sent a **nervous** tremor through her. Evelyn understood there was a **mystery** she couldn't **misunderstand**, a **tyrannical** force at play that would **afflict** them all. This was no typical **university** prank, but a **fanatical** plot that felt **far** too serious; a **valkyrie**-like figure, a **jester**-esque **heathen**, seemed to **conceive** a plan to **kill** and **sucking** their resources, threatening to **overcook** the whole operation. Her instincts screamed **obstacle**, demanding she **advance** as she felt her entire world **disjointing**, like a machine suddenly **jackknifed**, its **numeral** gears seizing with unbearable **torque** from a **ganging** conspiracy.

In the industrial **graveyard** of an old train yard, amidst towering **locomotives** and piles of **landfill** **junk**, an **irresistible** urge to confront the source of the chaos took hold. A sudden **fire** erupted, forcing her to instinctively **showjumped** over a burning wreckage, the **baroque** twisting metal like a monstrous sculpture. She felt the **giveback** of her old training, her **knuckle** cracking as she delivered a precise blow, watching a hidden attacker **get** toppled from a **plinth**-like pile of debris, their **jaw** clenching in surprise. Under the **aegis** of the night, she saw the **unitary** purpose in her fight, determined to remain **uninjured**, battling her way through the **trench**-like alleys, the air thick with the smell of smoke and the promise of victory. She used a broken **toothpicks**-like shard of metal as a makeshift weapon, leveraging its **benefit**, ready to **rise**, dressed in a tough **gabardine** coat, in this **international** **naval** affair.

After the chaos, a quiet sense of **utopia** settled. Evelyn sat in her study, a lone **candelabrum** casting flickering shadows over her **tarot** cards and scattered **newspapers**, sifting through the **nuance** of the recent events. She understood the deeper **instructions** now, feeling **keen** and resolute for the future. Her movements, once tentative, now had the fluid **locomotion** of her past **kickboxing** glory. She decided to **postpone** retirement, finding new purpose, a **panegyric** to resilience. The **veiling** of the past was lifted, revealing a future she could **find** and **approve**, **neither** a complete victory **nor** a total defeat, just an **outtake** from a larger story. She peeled a **chicken** off a rotisserie, considering the future, her old **sweater** worn and comforting, the puzzle of the past a resolved **jigsaw**, leaving only the comforting **nubble** of certainty and the protective **netting** of her renewed strength, ready to **crawl** through any obstacle, even if it meant becoming a **lumberjacks**-level problem solver.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, slick graphic novel art style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A worldly woman in her late 50s with prominent laugh lines, a sharp bob haircut with silver streaks, and wearing a tailored gabardine blazer. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The woman sitting thoughtfully under a circular array of umbrellas at an outdoor cider bar, observing a golf course, looking pensive. (Medium shot) TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The woman standing, her nervous expression hardening as a shadowy figure lunges towards her from the shadows. (Dynamic low angle) BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The woman executing a kickboxing move, deflecting an attacker amidst a chaotic, industrial setting with locomotives in the background and a fire burning. (Wide shot, action perspective) BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The woman sitting by a candelabrum, looking at tarot cards and newspapers, a thoughtful, determined expression, having achieved a quiet utopia. (Close-up on her face, slightly off-center). cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting.

definitions.definition: Concerned with human or earthly matters, physical as opposed to spiritual.
Concerned with secular rather than sacred matters.
Sophisticated, especially because of surfeit; versed in the ways of the world.
In a worldly manner.
To fold in the middle, as a jackknife does.
definitions.example: The cat jackknifed in the air and landed gracefully on its feet.

definitions.definition: To cause a semi-trailer truck to fold like a jackknife in a traffic accident.
definitions.example: Before I knew what was happening, I'd jack-knifed the truck.

definitions.definition: A ball game played by individuals competing against one another in which the object is to hit a ball into each of a series of (usually 18 or nine) holes in the minimum number of strokes.
The letter G in the ICAO spelling alphabet.
To play the game of golf.
To write something in as few characters as possible (e.g. in code golf, regex golf)
Cloth-covered frame used for protection against rain or sun.
definitions.example: Quick, grab that umbrella before you get rained on!

definitions.definition: Generally, anything that provides protection.
definitions.example: The fighters provide a defensive air umbrella over the battle group.

definitions.definition: Something that covers a wide range of concepts, purposes, groups, etc.
definitions.example: The test facility was established under the umbrella of the company's quality program.

definitions.definition: The main body of a jellyfish, excluding the tentacles.
definitions.example: Jellyfish are composed of more than 90% water and most of their umbrella mass is made up of gelatinous material.

definitions.definition: An umbrella-shaped reflector with a white or silvery inner surface, used to diffuse a nearby light.
To cover or protect, as if by an umbrella.
To form the dome shape of an open umbrella.
To move like a jellyfish.
Short for circular letter.
A sleeveless cloak cut from a circular pattern.
A shuttle bus with a circular route.
To distribute circulars to or at.
To extend in a circular direction.
Of or relating to a circle.
In the shape of, or moving in a circle.
Circuitous or roundabout.
Referring back to itself, so as to prevent computation or comprehension; infinitely recursive.
definitions.example: Your dictionary defines "brave" as "courageous", and "courageous" as "brave". That's a circular definition.

definitions.definition: Distributed to a large number of persons.
Perfect; complete.
Adhering to a fixed circle of legends; cyclic; hence, mean; inferior.
That which flies, as a bird or insect.
A machine that flies.
An airplane pilot.
A person who travels by airplane.
A leaflet, often for advertising.
The part of a spinning machine that twists the thread as it takes it to and winds it on the bobbin
An arch that connects a flying buttress into the structure it supports.
(acrobatics, cheerleading, synchronized swimming) The person who is lifted and/or thrown by another person or persons.
A stray shot away from the group on a target.
A standard rectangular step of a staircase (as opposed to a winder).
A female kangaroo; a roo; a doe; a jill.
A leap or jump.
A risky investment or other venture.
An alcoholic, often sparkling (carbonated) beverage made from fermented apples; hard cider; apple cider

A non-alcoholic still beverage consisting of the juice of early-harvest apples, usually unfiltered and still containing pulp; apple cider; sweet cider (without pulp such a beverage is called apple juice).
definitions.example: She liked an aged cider. He liked a harder cider.

definitions.definition: A non-alcoholic carbonated beverage made from apples.
(in Korea) A non-alcoholic drink, normally carbonated; equivalent to soft drink.
A cup, glass or serving of any of these beverages.
(chiefly British) An area of land; a district, region.

A set region of land having particular human occupation or agreed limits, especially inhabited by members of the same race, speakers of the same language etc., or associated with a given person, occupation, species etc.
The territory of a nation, especially an independent nation state or formerly independent nation; a political entity asserting ultimate authority over a geographical area; a sovereign state.
(usually preceded by “the”) A rural area, as opposed to a town or city; the countryside.
The rock through which a vein runs.
The female genitalia, especially the vagina.
From or in the countryside or connected with it.
Of or connected to country music.
A style of popular music that originated in the folk music of the rural population of the southern and western United States and characterized by string-band instruments and simple melodies.
The process or result of being gradually decomposed.
A deterioration of condition; loss of status or fortune.
To deteriorate, to get worse, to lose strength or health, to decline in quality.
definitions.example: The pair loved to take pictures in the decaying hospital on forty-third street.

definitions.definition: (of organic material) To rot, to go bad.
definitions.example: The cat's body decayed rapidly.

definitions.definition: (of an unstable atom) To change by undergoing fission, by emitting radiation, or by capturing or losing one or more electrons.
(of a quantum system) To undergo optical decay, that is, to relax to a less excited state, usually by emitting a photon or phonon.
Loss of airspeed due to drag.
To cause to rot or deteriorate.
definitions.example: The extreme humidity decayed the wooden sculptures in the museum's collection in a matter of years.

definitions.definition: In spite of what preceded; yet.
To cook for too long or at too high a temperature.
To do something to excess; to overdo.
definitions.example: The runner overcooked it on the first lap, and ended up finishing last.

definitions.definition: Appropriate for or suggestive of singing.
Expressive of emotion.
One that plays
One who is playful; one without serious aims; an idler; a trifler.
A significant participant.
definitions.example: He thought he could become a player, at least at the state level.

definitions.definition: A person who plays the field rather than having a long-term sexual relationship.
Something that prevents, hampers, or hinders.
definitions.example: Age is often a handicap.

definitions.definition: An allowance of a certain amount of time or distance in starting, granted in a race (or other contest of skill) to the competitor possessing disadvantages; or an additional weight or other hindrance imposed upon the one possessing advantages, in order to equalize, as much as possible, the chances of success.
definitions.example: A handicap in chess often involves removal of the queen's rook.

definitions.definition: (sometimes considered offensive) The disadvantage itself, in particular physical or mental disadvantages of people.
A race or similar contest in which there is an allowance of time, distance, weight, or other advantage, to equalize the chances of the competitors.
(card game) An old card game, similar to lanterloo.
To encumber with a handicap in any contest.
(by extension) To place at disadvantage.
definitions.example: The candidate was handicapped by her lack of experience.

definitions.definition: To estimate betting odds.
definitions.example: Grandpa Andy would buy the racing form the day ahead of time so he could handicap the race before he even arrived at the track.

definitions.definition: To understand incorrectly, while believing one has understood correctly.

Institution of higher education (typically accepting students from the age of about 17 or 18, depending on country, but in some exceptional cases able to take younger students) where subjects are studied and researched in depth and degrees are offered.
definitions.example: The only reason why I haven't gone to university is because I can't afford it.

definitions.definition: Having an extreme, irrational zeal or enthusiasm for a specific cause.
Distant; remote in space.
definitions.example: He went to a far land.

definitions.definition: Remote in time.
definitions.example: the far future

definitions.definition: Long.
More remote of two.
definitions.example: He moved to the far end of the state. She remained at this end.

definitions.definition: Extreme, as measured from some central or neutral position.
definitions.example: They are on the far right on this issue.

definitions.definition: Extreme, as a difference in nature or quality.
Outside the currently selected segment in a segmented memory architecture.
definitions.example: far heap; far memory; far pointer

definitions.definition: To, from or over a great distance in space, time or other extent.
definitions.example: He built a time machine and travelled far into the future.

definitions.definition: Very much; by a great amount.
definitions.example: He was far richer than we'd thought.

definitions.definition: A day that is an exact number of years (to the day) since a given significant event occurred. Often preceded by an ordinal number indicating the number of years.
definitions.example: Today is the fiftieth anniversary of the end of the war.

definitions.definition: (loosely) A day subsequent in time to a given event by some significant period other than a year (especially as prefixed by the amount of time in question).
Of, or relating to tyranny or a tyrant.
Despotic, oppressive or authoritarian.
A lack of moisture.
The degree to which something is dry.
A formal speech or opus publicly praising someone or something.
Someone who writes or delivers such a speech.
Panegyrical
One who jests, jokes or teases.
A person in colourful garb and fool's cap who amused a medieval and early modern royal or noble court.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the Southeast Asian genus Symbrenthia.
An adherent of the Germanic neo-pagan faith of Heathenry.
Pertaining or adhering to the Germanic neo-pagan faith Heathenry.
A person who does not follow a Christian religion; a pagan.
(by extension) An uncultured or uncivilized person, philistine.
Not adhering to Christian religion; pagan.
(by extension) Uncultured; uncivilized; savage, philistine.
A unitary council.
A unitary matrix or operator.
Having the quality of oneness.
(of a system of government or administration) That concentrates power in a single body, rather than sharing it with more local bodies.
definitions.example: a unitary authority

definitions.definition: (of an algebra) That contains an identity element.
(of a matrix or operator) Whose inverse is equal to its adjoint.
One or many people or objects that have not suffered injury.
definitions.example: The driver was hurt and went to hospital whilst the uninjured passengers went home.

definitions.definition: That did not suffer injury.
definitions.example: Of the three, two were hurt and went to hospital, but the uninjured one made her way home.

definitions.definition: To develop an idea; to form in the mind; to plan; to devise; to originate.
To understand (someone).
To become pregnant (with).
definitions.example: Assisted procreation can help those trying to conceive.

definitions.definition: Impossile to resist.
Compellingly attractive.
(usually in the negative) (Good) condition, form, or order; fettle.
A hand of playing cards which is useless; a dead man's hand.
Any of the joints between the phalanges of the fingers.
(by extension) A mechanical joint.
A cut of meat.
The curved part of the cushion at the entrance to the pockets on a cue sports table.
The kneejoint of a quadruped, especially of a calf; formerly used of the kneejoint of a human being.
The joint of a plant.
A convex portion of a vessel's figure where a sudden change of shape occurs, as in a canal boat, where a nearly vertical side joins a nearly flat bottom.
A contrivance, usually of brass or iron, and furnished with points, worn to protect the hand, to add force to a blow, and to disfigure the person struck; a knuckle duster.
definitions.example: brass knuckles

definitions.definition: The rounded point where a flat changes to a slope on a piste.
To apply pressure, or rub or massage with one's knuckles.
definitions.example: He knuckled the sleep from his eyes.

definitions.definition: To bend the fingers.
To touch one's forehead as a mark of respect.
To yield.
Any land or marine reptile of the order Testudines, characterised by a protective shell enclosing its body. See also tortoise.
(specifically) A marine reptile of that order.
An Ancient Roman attack method, where the shields held by the soldiers hide them, not only left, right, front and back, but also from above.
A type of robot having a domed case (and so resembling the reptile), used in education, especially for making line drawings by means of a computer program.
An on-screen cursor that serves the same function as a turtle for drawing.
The curved plate in which the form is held in a type-revolving cylinder press.
A small element towards the end of a list of items to be bubble sorted, and thus tending to take a long time to be swapped into its correct position. Compare rabbit.
A breakdancing move consisting of a float during which the dancer's weight shifts from one hand to the other, producing rotation or a circular "walk".
A low stand for a lamp etc.
To flip over onto the back or top; to turn upside down.
To turn and swim upside down.
To hunt turtles, especially in the water.
To build up a large defense force and strike only punctually, rather than going for an offensive strategy.
To render disjoint; to remove a connection, linkage, or intersection.
definitions.example: to disjoint limbs; to disjoint bones; to disjoint poultry by carving

definitions.definition: To break the natural order and relations of; to make incoherent.
definitions.example: a disjointed speech

definitions.definition: To fall into pieces.
A minor distinction.
Subtlety or fine detail.
definitions.example: Understanding the basics is easy, but appreciating the nuances takes years.

definitions.definition: To apply a nuance to; to change or redefine in a subtle way.
Of sinews and tendons.
Of nerves.
To make opaque.
A rebate.
A reduction in pay or conditions as a result of unfavourable economic conditions.
Unfortunate, marked by misfortune.
Inauspicious.
Having ill luck.
Bringing ill luck.
Someone from the continent.
A member of the Continental army.
Paper scrip (paper money) issued by the continental congress, largely worthless by the end of the war.
(by extension) The smallest amount; a whit; a jot.
definitions.example: I don't care a continental!

definitions.definition: Of or relating to a continent or continents.
definitions.example: continental drift

definitions.definition: Of the mainland, as opposed to an island offshore
definitions.example: continental Europe

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the UK) Relating to, or characteristic of, continental Europe
definitions.example: continental breakfast

definitions.definition: Of or relating to the confederated colonies collectively, in the time of the Revolutionary War.
definitions.example: continental money

definitions.definition: Full of, or edged with, reeds.
(of a sound or voice) High and thin in tone.
(of a person) Tall and thin.
Imperfectly welded together in masses of rods.
definitions.example: reedy iron

definitions.definition: Anything that is found (usually valuable), as objects on an archeological site or a person with talent.
The act of finding.
To encounter or discover by accident; to happen upon.
To encounter or discover something being searched for; to locate.
definitions.example: I found my car keys. They were under the couch.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To discover by study or experiment direct to an object or end.
definitions.example: Water is found to be a compound substance.

definitions.definition: To gain, as the object of desire or effort.
definitions.example: to find leisure; to find means

definitions.definition: To attain to; to arrive at; to acquire.
definitions.example: Looks like he found a new vehicle for himself!

definitions.definition: To point out.
definitions.example: He kept finding faults with my work.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To decide that, to discover that, to form the opinion that.
definitions.example: I find your argument unsatisfactory.

definitions.definition: To arrive at, as a conclusion; to determine as true; to establish.
definitions.example: to find a verdict; to find a true bill (of indictment) against an accused person

definitions.definition: To supply; to furnish.
definitions.example: to find food for workmen

definitions.definition: To provide for
definitions.example: He finds his nephew in money.

definitions.definition: To determine or judge.
definitions.example: The jury finds for the defendant.

definitions.definition: To discover game.
A small, usually wooden, stick, often pointed at both ends, for removing food residue from between the teeth.
An advantage; help or aid from something.
definitions.example: It was for her benefit.   His benefit was free beer.

definitions.definition: A payment made in accordance with an insurance policy or a public assistance scheme.
An event such as a performance, given to raise funds for some cause.
Beneficence; liberality
To be or to provide a benefit to.
To receive a benefit (from); to be a beneficiary.
Ornate, intricate, decorated, laden with detail.
Complex and beautiful, despite an outward irregularity.
Chiseled from stone, or shaped from wood, in a garish, crooked, twisted, or slanted sort of way, grotesque.
Embellished with figures and forms such that every level of relief gives way to more details and contrasts.
To forcibly stop and seize control of some vehicle in order to rob it or to reach a destination (especially an airplane, truck or a boat).
To seize control of some process or resource to achieve a purpose other than its originally intended one.
To seize control of a networked computer by means of infecting it with a worm or other malware, thereby turning it into a zombie.
To change software settings without a user's knowledge so as to force that user to visit a certain web site (to hijack a browser).
To introduce an amendment deleting the contents of a bill and inserting entirely new provisions.
A person who traffics in commodities for profit.
The owner or operator of a retail business.
A trading vessel; a merchantman.
A supercargo.
As a resident of a region, to buy goods from a non-resident and sell them to another non-resident.
definitions.example: a merchanting service

definitions.definition: Someone who moves about in roving fashion looking for plunder.
definitions.example: a band of marauders

definitions.definition: By extension anything which marauds.
To move, or appear to move, physically upwards relative to the ground.
To increase in value or standing.
To begin; to develop.
To go up; to ascend; to climb.
definitions.example: to rise a hill

definitions.definition: To cause to go up or ascend.
definitions.example: to rise a fish, or cause it to come to the surface of the water

definitions.definition: To retire; to give up a siege.
To come; to offer itself.
To be lifted, or capable of being lifted, from the imposing stone without dropping any of the type; said of a form.
A (usually self-sustaining) chemical reaction involving the bonding of oxygen with carbon or other fuel, with the production of heat and the presence of flame or smouldering.
An instance of this chemical reaction, especially when intentionally created and maintained in a specific location to a useful end (such as a campfire or a hearth fire).
definitions.example: We sat about the fire singing songs and telling tales.

definitions.definition: The occurrence, often accidental, of fire in a certain place, causing damage and danger.
definitions.example: During hot and dry summers many fires in forests are caused by regardlessly discarded cigarette butts.

definitions.definition: The aforementioned chemical reaction of burning, considered one of the Classical elements or basic elements of alchemy.
A heater or stove used in place of a real fire (such as an electric fire).
The elements necessary to start a fire.
definitions.example: The fire was laid and needed to be lit.

definitions.definition: The bullets or other projectiles fired from a gun.
definitions.example: The fire from the enemy guns kept us from attacking.

definitions.definition: Strength of passion, whether love or hate.
Liveliness of imagination or fancy; intellectual and moral enthusiasm.
Splendour; brilliancy; lustre; hence, a star.
A severe trial; anything inflaming or provoking.
Red coloration in a piece of opal.
To take part in showjumping.
A knitted jacket or jersey, usually of thick wool, worn by athletes before or after exercise.
A similar garment worn for warmth.
One who sweats (produces sweat).
One who or that which causes to sweat.
A diaphoretic remedy.
A middleman who subcontracted piece work in the tailoring trade.
One who sweats coins, i.e. removes small portions by shaking them.
A London street ruffian in Queen Anne's time who prodded weak passengers with his sword-point.
To dress in a sweater.

A mythological shield associated with the Greek deities Zeus and Athena (and their Roman counterparts Jupiter and Minerva) shown as a short cloak made of goatskin worn on the shoulders, more as an emblem of power and protection than a military shield. The aegis of Athena or Minerva is usually shown with a border of snakes and with the head of Medusa in the center.
Usually as under the aegis: guidance, protection; endorsement, sponsorship.
To sanction officially; to ratify; to confirm; to set as satisfactory.
definitions.example: Although we may disagree with it, we must nevertheless approve the sentence handed down by the court-martial.

definitions.definition: To regard as good; to commend; to be pleased with; to think well of.
definitions.example: We approve the measure of the administration, for it is an excellent decision.

definitions.definition: To make proof of; to demonstrate; to prove or show practically.
(followed by "of") To consider worthy (to); to be pleased (with); to accept.
definitions.example: Her mother never approves of any of her boyfriends. She thinks nobody is good enough for her little girl.

definitions.definition: To show to be worthy; to demonstrate the merits of.
A long, narrow ditch or hole dug in the ground.
A narrow excavation as used in warfare, as a cover for besieging or emplaced forces.
A pit, usually rectangular with smooth walls and floor, excavated during an archaeological investigation.
A trench coat.
(usually followed by upon) To invade, especially with regard to the rights or the exclusive authority of another; to encroach.
(infantry) To excavate an elongated pit for protection of soldiers and or equipment, usually perpendicular to the line of sight toward the enemy.
To excavate an elongated and often narrow pit.
To have direction; to aim or tend.
To cut; to form or shape by cutting; to make by incision, hewing, etc.
To cut furrows or ditches in.
definitions.example: to trench land for the purpose of draining it

definitions.definition: To dig or cultivate very deeply, usually by digging parallel contiguous trenches in succession, filling each from the next.
definitions.example: to trench a garden for certain crops

definitions.definition: A ride on somebody's back or shoulders.
An act or instance of piggybacking.
To attach or append something to another (usually larger) object or event.
definitions.example: The popular host can’t claim credit for the trade, though. The idea wasn’t his. He piggybacked off another successful investor who had a history of picking winners.

definitions.definition: To obtain a wireless internet connection by bringing one's own computer within the range of another's wireless connection without that subscriber's permission or knowledge.
To utilize "last-mile" wiring rented from a larger owner ISP by a smaller ISP.
To carry someone on the back or shoulders.
To transport (a lorry/truck) on a flatbed railway waggon
To enter a secured area at the same time along with someone having authorized access; to tailgate
On somebody's back or shoulders.
definitions.example: a piggyback ride

definitions.definition: Pertaining to transportation of goods where one transportation unit is carried on the back of something else. For example, a truck on a train.
On somebody's back or shoulders.
definitions.example: to ride piggyback

definitions.definition: A block or slab upon which a column, pedestal, statue or other structure is based.
The bottom course of stones or bricks supporting a wall.
A base or pedestal beneath a cabinet.
One of the bones, usually bearing teeth, which form the framework of the mouth.
The part of the face below the mouth.
definitions.example: His jaw dropped in amazement.

definitions.definition: Anything resembling the jaw of an animal in form or action; especially plural, the mouth or way of entrance.
definitions.example: the jaws of a pass; the jaws of darkness; the jaws of death.

definitions.definition: A notch or opening.
A notched or forked part, adapted for holding an object in place.
definitions.example: the jaw of a railway-car pedestal.

definitions.definition: One of a pair of opposing parts which are movable towards or from each other, for grasping or crushing anything between them.
definitions.example: the jaws of a vise; the jaws of a stone-crushing machine.

definitions.definition: The inner end of a boom or gaff, hollowed in a half circle so as to move freely on a mast.
Impudent or abusive talk.
Axle guard.
The curved part of the cushion marking the entry to the pocket.
To assail or abuse by scolding.
To scold; to clamor.
To talk; to converse.
(of a ball) To stick in the jaws of a pocket.
A saw with fine teeth and a narrow blade which can cut curves in wood or metal.
A jigsaw puzzle.
To cut something using a jigsaw.
A tract of land in which the dead are buried.
(by extension) A final storage place for collections of things that are no longer useful or useable.
Offspring.
Lineage.
A difficult return or block of a shot.
Something gained; an acquisition.
(ditransitive) To obtain; to acquire.
definitions.example: I'm going to get a computer tomorrow from the discount store.

definitions.definition: To receive.
definitions.example: He got a severe reprimand for that.

definitions.definition: (in a perfect construction, with present-tense meaning) To have. See usage notes.
definitions.example: I've got a concert ticket for you.

definitions.definition: To fetch, bring, take.
definitions.example: Can you get my bag from the living-room, please?

definitions.definition: To become, or cause oneself to become.
definitions.example: I'm getting hungry; how about you?

definitions.definition: To cause to become; to bring about.
definitions.example: I can't get these boots off (or on).

definitions.definition: To cause to do.
definitions.example: I can't get it to work.

definitions.definition: To cause to come or go or move.
definitions.example: I got him to his room.

definitions.definition: To cause to be in a certain status or position.
definitions.example: Get him here at once.

definitions.definition: (with various prepositions, such as into, over, or behind; for specific idiomatic senses see individual entries get into, get over, etc.) To adopt, assume, arrive at, or progress towards (a certain position, location, state).
definitions.example: I'm getting into a muddle.

definitions.definition: To cover (a certain distance) while travelling.
definitions.example: to get a mile

definitions.definition: To begin (doing something or to do something).
definitions.example: After lunch we got chatting.

definitions.definition: To take or catch (a scheduled transportation service).
definitions.example: I normally get the 7:45 train.

definitions.definition: To respond to (a telephone call, a doorbell, etc).
definitions.example: Can you get that call, please? I'm busy.

definitions.definition: (followed by infinitive) To be able, be permitted, or have the opportunity (to do something desirable or ironically implied to be desirable).
definitions.example: Great. I get to clean the toilets today.

definitions.definition: To understand. (compare get it)
definitions.example: I don't get what you mean by "fun". This place sucks!

definitions.definition: To be told; be the recipient of (a question, comparison, opinion, etc.).
definitions.example: "You look just like Helen Mirren." / "I get that a lot."

definitions.definition: To be. Used to form the passive of verbs.
definitions.example: He got bitten by a dog.

definitions.definition: To become ill with or catch (a disease).
definitions.example: I went on holiday and got malaria.

definitions.definition: To catch out, trick successfully.
definitions.example: He keeps calling pretending to be my boss—it gets me every time.

definitions.definition: To perplex, stump.
definitions.example: That question's really got me.

definitions.definition: To find as an answer.
definitions.example: What did you get for question four?

definitions.definition: To bring to reckoning; to catch (as a criminal); to effect retribution.
definitions.example: I'm gonna get him for that.

definitions.definition: To hear completely; catch.
definitions.example: Sorry, I didn't get that. Could you repeat it?

definitions.definition: To getter.
definitions.example: I put the getter into the container to get the gases.

definitions.definition: To beget (of a father).
To learn; to commit to memory; to memorize; sometimes with out.
definitions.example: to get a lesson;  to get out one's Greek lesson

definitions.definition: Used with a personal pronoun to indicate that someone is being pretentious or grandiose.
definitions.example: Get her with her new hairdo.

definitions.definition: To go, to leave; to scram.
To kill.
definitions.example: They’re coming to get you, Barbara.

definitions.definition: To make acquisitions; to gain; to profit.
To measure.
definitions.example: Did you get her temperature?

definitions.definition: A world in which everything and everyone works in perfect harmony.
A domestic fowl, Gallus gallus, especially when young.
The meat from this bird eaten as food.
The young of any bird; a chick.
A coward.
A young or inexperienced person.
A young, attractive, slim man, usually having little body hair; compare chickenhawk.
The game of dare.
A simple dance in which the movements of a chicken are imitated.
Cowardly.
definitions.example: Why do you refuse to fight? Huh, I guess you're just too chicken.

definitions.definition: The act of instructing, teaching, or furnishing with information or knowledge.
definitions.example: Instruction will be provided on how to handle difficult customers.

definitions.definition: An instance of the information or knowledge so furnished.
An order or command.
A single operation of a processor defined by an instruction set architecture.
A set of directions provided by a manufacturer for the users of a product or service.
To dress in, or decorate with, a veil.
To conceal as with a veil.
definitions.example: The forest fire was veiled by smoke, but I could hear it clearly.

definitions.definition: The act of covering with a veil.
Material for making veils.
To make cold, to sharpen.
Often with a prepositional phrase, or with to and an infinitive: showing a quick and ardent responsiveness or willingness; eager, enthusiastic, interested.
definitions.example: I’m keen on computers.

definitions.definition: Fierce, intense, vehement.
definitions.example: This boy has a keen appetite.

definitions.definition: Having a fine edge or point; sharp.
Acute of mind, having or expressing mental acuteness; penetrating, sharp.
Acrimonious, bitter, piercing.
definitions.example: keen satire or sarcasm

definitions.definition: Of cold, wind, etc.: cutting, penetrating, piercing, sharp.
definitions.example: a keen wind

definitions.definition: Of prices, extremely low as to be competitive.
Marvelous.
definitions.example: I just got this peachy keen new dress.

definitions.definition: Brave, courageous; audacious, bold.
A hybrid martial art derived from Muay Thai, karate and especially boxing during the 1960s to 1970s; more generally, any stand-up combat sport that combines kicks and punches.
definitions.example: The Japanese kickboxing gym, recently opened in the Thai capital, had been a great success (Black Belt Magazine, March 1973, p. 13)

definitions.definition: A person who has knowledge; an informed party.
Having knowledge, especially of a particular subject.
Educated and well informed.
To cause (someone) pain, suffering or distress.
To strike or cast down; to overthrow.
To make low or humble.
A forward move; improvement or progression.
definitions.example: an advance in health or knowledge

definitions.definition: An amount of money or credit, especially given as a loan, or paid before it is due; an advancement.
An addition to the price; rise in price or value.
definitions.example: an advance on the prime cost of goods

definitions.definition: (in the plural) An opening approach or overture, especially of an unwelcome or sexual nature.
To promote or advantage.
To move forward in space or time.
To raise, be raised.
Completed before necessary or a milestone event.
definitions.example: He made an advance payment on the prior shipment to show good faith.

definitions.definition: Preceding
definitions.example: The advance man came a month before the candidate.

definitions.definition: Forward
definitions.example: The scouts found a site for an advance base.

definitions.definition: Similarly not.
definitions.example: Just as you would not correct it, neither would I.

definitions.definition: Not either one of two.
definitions.example: I've tried on both shirts, but neither fits properly.

definitions.definition: Not either (used with nor).
definitions.example: Neither now, nor ever will he forsake his mother.

definitions.definition: A drug or substance that acts upon the nerves.
Having the quality of acting upon or affecting the nerves; quieting nervous excitement.
Something that impedes, stands in the way of, or holds up progress
definitions.example: A big obstacle to understanding the manual was that it had been poorly translated from the Japanese.

definitions.definition: A portion of a recording (a take) that is not included in the final version of a film or a musical album, often because it contains a mistake.
definitions.example: The DVD for that movie has ten minutes worth of outtakes.

definitions.definition: A complete version of a recording or film that is dropped in favour of another version, reject.
An opening for outward discharge; a vent.
To take out, remove.
To except.
A person whose work is to fell trees.
A lumberjacket.
The state of being secluded from the presence, sight, or knowledge of others.
definitions.example: I need my privacy, so please stay out of my room.

definitions.definition: Freedom from unwanted or undue disturbance of one private life.
definitions.example: It takes a village to rob one of a sense of privacy.

definitions.definition: Freedom from damaging publicity, public scrutiny, surveillance, and disclosure of personal information, usually by a government or a private organization.
definitions.example: Privacy is assumed by many to be among common-law rights.

definitions.definition: A place of seclusion.
A relationship between parties seen as being a result of their mutual interest or participation in a given transaction, contract etc.; Privity.
Secrecy.
A private matter; a secret.
To delay or put off an event, appointment etc.
The act of jostling someone or something; push, shove.
The action of a jostling crowd.
To bump into or brush against while in motion; to push aside.
To move through by pushing and shoving.
To be close to or in physical contact with.
To contend or vie in order to acquire something.
To pick or attempt to pick pockets.
A corridor in a building that connects rooms.
A person whose job is to manage something, such as a business, a restaurant, or a sports team.
The head coach.
An administrator, for a singer or group.
(computer software) A window or application whose purpose is to give the user the control over some aspect of the system.
definitions.example: a file manager; a task manager; Program Manager

definitions.definition: A candle holder.
A device for measuring time, such as a watch or clock.
Any of the female attendants of Odin, figures said to guide fallen warriors from the battlefield to Valhalla.
definitions.example: Wagner's “Der Ring des Nibelungen” (1853) famously features valkyries.

definitions.definition: A large room, especially one used to receive and entertain guests.
A gathering of people for a social or intellectual meeting.
An art gallery or exhibition; especially the Paris salon or autumn salon.
A beauty salon or similar establishment.

A rotational or twisting effect of a force; a moment of force, defined for measurement purposes as an equivalent straight line force multiplied by the distance from the axis of rotation (SI unit newton metre or Nm; imperial unit pound-foot or lb·ft, not to be confused with the foot pound-force, commonly "foot-pound", a unit of work or energy)
To twist or turn something.
Of or relating to a navy.
Of or relating to ships in general.
definitions.example: naval architect

definitions.definition: A small knob or lump.
To beat or bruise with the fist.
Something that acts as, or looks like, a net.
A symbol that is not a word and represents a number, such as the Arabic numerals 1, 2, 3 and the Roman numerals I, V, X, L.
A word representing a number.
A card whose rank is a number (usually including the ace as 1).
definitions.example: Jacks, queens, and kings are not numerals.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to numbers; numerical.
The power unit of a train that pulls the coaches or wagons.
A traction engine
A cheer characterized by a slow beginning and a progressive increase in speed
A country which drives the world economy by having a high level of imports. (i.e. The United States).
A type of woolen cloth with a diagonal ribbed texture on one side.
definitions.example: The merchant found gabardines with finer ribs sold better here.

definitions.definition: A similar fabric, made from cotton.
A gaberdine (garment).
A yellow robe that Jews in England were compelled to wear in the year 1189 as a mark of distinction.
To go; walk; proceed.
To attach similar items together to form a larger unit.
To participate in a gangbang.
definitions.example: We all got to gangbang the porn star.

definitions.definition: To beat one or a smaller amount of people as a gang.
To belong to a gang; to commit criminal acts as part of a gang.
definitions.example: It broke my heart to see him out there gangbanging.

definitions.definition: A leader used to attach a fishhook to the main line, especially in commercial fishing
Something secret or unexplainable; an unknown.
definitions.example: The truth behind the events remains a mystery.

definitions.definition: Someone or something with an obscure or puzzling nature.
definitions.example: That man is a mystery.

definitions.definition: A secret or mystical meaning.
A religious truth not understandable by the application of human reason alone (without divine aid).
(obsolete outside Eastern Orthodoxy) A sacrament.
(chiefly in plural) A secret religious celebration, admission to which was usually through initiation.
definitions.example: the Eleusinian mysteries

definitions.definition: A particular event or series of events in the life of Christ.
definitions.example: The second decade of the Rosary concerns the Sorrowful mysteries, such as the crucifixion and the crowning with thorns.

definitions.definition: A craft, art or trade; specifically a guild of craftsmen.
The act of moving slowly on hands and knees etc, or with frequent stops.
A rapid swimming stroke with alternate overarm strokes and a fluttering kick.
A very slow pace.
definitions.example: My computer has slowed down to a crawl since I installed that software package.

definitions.definition: A piece of horizontally or vertically scrolling text overlaid on the main image.
To creep; to move slowly on hands and knees, or by dragging the body along the ground.
definitions.example: Clutching my wounded side, I crawled back to the trench.

definitions.definition: To move forward slowly, with frequent stops.
definitions.example: The rush-hour traffic crawled around the bypass.

definitions.definition: To act in a servile manner.
definitions.example: Don't come crawling to me with your useless apologies!

definitions.definition: (with "with") See crawl with.
To feel a swarming sensation.
definitions.example: The horrible sight made my skin crawl.

definitions.definition: To swim using the crawl stroke.
definitions.example: I think I'll crawl the next hundred metres.

definitions.definition: To move over an area on hands and knees.
definitions.example: The baby crawled the entire second floor.

definitions.definition: To visit while becoming inebriated.
definitions.example: They crawled the downtown bars.

definitions.definition: To visit files or web sites in order to index them for searching.
definitions.example: Yahoo Search has updated its Slurp Crawler to crawl web sites faster and more efficiently.

definitions.definition: Someone who has represented their country in a particular sport.
definitions.example: The United team includes five England internationals.

definitions.definition: A game or contest between two or more nations.
A transnational organization of political parties of similar ideology.
Of or having to do with more than one nation.
Between or among nations
definitions.example: an international discussion

definitions.definition: Participated in by two or more nations
definitions.example: an international competition

definitions.definition: Common to, or affecting, two or more nations.
definitions.example: an international rule

definitions.definition: Serving two or more nations
definitions.example: an international airport

definitions.definition: Of or concerning the association called the International.
Independent of national boundaries; common to all people.
definitions.example: The atmosphere is an international resource.

definitions.definition: Foreign; of another nation.
definitions.example: an international student

definitions.definition: The act of killing.
definitions.example: The assassin liked to make a clean kill, and thus favored small arms over explosives.

definitions.definition: Specifically, the death blow.
definitions.example: The hunter delivered the kill with a pistol shot to the head.

definitions.definition: The result of killing; that which has been killed.
definitions.example: The fox dragged its kill back to its den.

definitions.definition: The grounding of the ball on the opponent's court, winning the rally.
To put to death; to extinguish the life of.
definitions.example: Smoking kills more people each year than alcohol and drugs combined.

definitions.definition: To render inoperative.
definitions.example: He killed the engine and turned off the headlights, but remained in the car, waiting.

definitions.definition: To stop, cease or render void; to terminate.
definitions.example: My computer wouldn't respond until I killed some of the running processes.

definitions.definition: To amaze, exceed, stun or otherwise incapacitate.
definitions.example: That joke always kills me.

definitions.definition: To cause great pain, discomfort or distress to.
definitions.example: These tight shoes are killing my feet.

definitions.definition: To produce feelings of dissatisfaction or revulsion in.
definitions.example: It kills me to learn how many poor people are practically starving in this country while rich moguls spend such outrageous amounts on useless luxuries.

definitions.definition: To use up or to waste.
definitions.example: He told the bartender, pointing at the bottle of scotch he planned to consume, "Leave it, I'm going to kill the bottle."

definitions.definition: To exert an overwhelming effect on.
definitions.example: Between the two of us, we killed the rest of the case of beer.

definitions.definition: To overpower, overwhelm or defeat.
definitions.example: The team had absolutely killed their traditional rivals, and the local sports bars were raucous with celebrations.

definitions.definition: To force a company out of business.
To produce intense pain.
definitions.example: You don't ever want to get rabies. The doctor will have to give you multiple shots and they really kill.

definitions.definition: To punish severely.
definitions.example: My parents are going to kill me!

definitions.definition: To strike (a ball, etc.) with such force and placement as to make a shot that is impossible to defend against, usually winning a point.
To cause (a ball, etc.) to be out of play, resulting in a stoppage of gameplay.
To succeed with an audience, especially in comedy.
To cause to assume the value zero.
(IRC) To disconnect (a user) involuntarily from the network.
To deadmelt.
To use the mouth and lips to pull in (a liquid, especially milk from the breast).
To perform such an action; to feed from a breast or teat.
To put the mouth or lips to (a breast, a mother etc.) to draw in milk.
To extract, draw in (a substance) from or out of something.
To work the lips and tongue on (an object) to extract moisture or nourishment; to absorb (something) in the mouth.
To pull (something) in a given direction, especially without direct contact.
To perform fellatio.
To be inferior or objectionable: a general term of disparagement, sometimes used with at to indicate a particular area of deficiency.
An act of sucking.
A sound or motion that sucks.
Still nourished by the mother's milk, as an infant; suckling.
(by extension) Young and inexperienced.
(singular or plural) A card game played in various different variations.
Any of the set of 78 playing cards (divided into five suits, including one of permanent trumps), often used for mystical divination.
A publication, usually published daily or weekly and usually printed on cheap, low-quality paper, containing news and other articles.
A quantity of or one of the types of paper on which newspapers are printed.
To cover with newspaper.
definitions.example: She newspapered one end of the room before painting the bookcase.

definitions.definition: To engage in the business of journalism (usually used only in the gerund, newspapering)
definitions.example: He newspapered his way through the South on the sports beat, avoiding dry towns.

definitions.definition: To harass in newspaper articles.
definitions.example: He was newspapered out of public life.

definitions.definition: The ability to move from place to place, or the act of doing so.
Self-powered motion by which a whole organism changes its location through walking, running, jumping, crawling, swimming or flying.
(often preceded by definite article) A dance, originally popular in the 1960s, in which the arms are used to mimic the motion of the connecting rods of a steam locomotive.
A site at which refuse is buried under layers of earth.
The material so disposed of.
To dispose of (garbage) by burying it at a landfill site.
Discarded or waste material; rubbish, trash.
A collection of miscellaneous items of little value.
Any narcotic drug, especially heroin.
The genitalia.
Salt beef.
Pieces of old cable or cordage, used for making gaskets, mats, swabs, etc., and when picked to pieces, forming oakum for filling the seams of ships.
A fragment of any solid substance; a thick piece; a chunk.
Material or resources of a kind lacking commercial value.
definitions.example: junk fish; junk trees

definitions.definition: To throw away.
To find something for very little money (meaning derived from the term junk shop)
definitions.example: (On Facebook, a record collector wrote:) "The newest addition to my Annette Hanshaw collection, I junked this beautiful flawless E-copy within walking distance from my house."

definitions.definition: A single unvaried tone of speech or a sound.
definitions.example: When Tima felt like her parents were treating her like a servant, she would speak in monotone and act as though she were a robot.

definitions.definition: A piece of writing in one strain throughout.
To speak in a monotone.
(of speech or a sound) Having a single unvaried pitch.
Being, or having the salient properties of, a monotone function.
definitions.example: The function f(x):=x^3 is monotone on \R, while g(x):=x^2 is not.
